title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR FERMENTED COOKED CASSAVA-BASED PRODUCTS (Africa) 1

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR FERMENTED COOKED CASSAVA-BASED PRODUCTS (Africa) 1
sections.summary: Document title and status: Regional Standard for Fermented Cooked Cassava-Based Products (Africa), CXS 334R-2020, adopted 2020 and amended 2022; the 2022 amendments include changes to wording in Section 7.3 on labelling of non-retail containers to reference CXS 346-2021.
Scope: applies to all fermented cooked cassava-based products intended for direct human consumption obtained from processing cassava roots (Manihot esculenta Crantz) and explicitly excludes gari.

Describes fermented cooked cassava-based products presented as cassava balls or sticks, produced from peeled, cut, soaked (for fermentation), pressed, dried and cooked cassava roots, and requires that if cooking occurs at point of consumption the uncooked product be stored and transported under time/temperature conditions that do not compromise safety.

Sets essential composition and quality factors for fermented cooked cassava-based products requiring they be clean and safe with characteristic moisture, free of abnormal smell/taste and foreign matter, specifies total acidity (as lactic acid) must be 0.1–0.5 g per 100 g, and restates presentation as cassava balls or sticks.

States general quality requirements that fermented cooked cassava-based products shall be clean and safe for consumption, have moisture characteristic of the product, and be free from abnormal smell/taste and foreign material such as insect fragments, rodent hairs, sand and dust.
Specifies total acidity (expressed as lactic acid) must be between 0.1 g and 0.5 g per 100 g of the fermented cassava preparation.
States that fermented cooked cassava-based products are presented in the form of cassava balls or sticks.
Declares that no food additives are permitted for use in this product.

Requires compliance with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CXS 193-1995), the Code of Practice for reduction of hydrocyanic acid in cassava (CXC 73-2013), and Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides applicable to these products.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and relevant Codex hygienic codes, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Specifies labelling requirements: the product name must be 'fermented cooked cassava-based products' followed by any product-specific/local name, labels should be placed on secondary packaging, and labelling of non-retail containers must follow CXS 346-2021 (with a footnote on pending determination of total hydrocyanic acid values by CCCF).
Requires the product name to be 'fermented cooked cassava-based products' followed by the product-specific or local name.
States that the label for products covered by this standard should be placed on the secondary packaging.

Requires that labelling of non-retail containers comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers in Foods (CXS 346-2021) and notes that values for total hydrocyanic acid will be determined pending completion of CCCF work.
Methods of analysis and sampling for this standard are not specified and are listed as 'To be developed'.
Provides bibliographic notes listing the Codex references cited in the standard (CXS 193-1995; CXC 73-2013; CXC 1-1969; CXG 21-1997; CXS 1-1985; CXS 346-2021).
